author: Ahmedabad Mirror
title: POWERHOUSE of ideas and energy
text:
AK Singh
Commissioner of Police, Ahmedabad
‘Bhadran’s capacity to work hard is unparalleled. I see him as a bigger national resource in coming time’
Deepan Bhadran’s domain knowledge is complete, be it law or the procedures. But more important is his knowledge in areas of emerging technologies, cyber world and forensics, especially collection of technical intelligence. His ability to think on his feet and bring out resources from his own armoury and put them into action makes him extraordinary.
We rely heavily on his ability to deal with all kinds of critical situations; whether it is a high-profile case or high-intensity law and order situation. He brings clarity to a complicated situation. His capacity to work hard is unparalleled. He can work at a stretch for three to four days without sleep and yet remain fresh and energetic with a smile on his face.
At the same time he is ever tender at heart and can get emotionally affected by the victim’s plight. He needs no motivation. He is ready to be drawn into a situation. He won’t rest until he has found a solution. Despite all the competencies, he keeps a low profile and never tries to take credit for anything. The new structures and systems he has created in the crime branch have helped the personnel solve many cases easily.
He has established a better technical cell in crime branch. Software systems like Eklavya, Pathik, and e-Gujcops are some of his initiatives. Though he is already contributing as the state and national resource, I see him as a bigger national resource in coming time as the national policy makers and decision makers are aware of his capabilities.
Deepan Bhadran
DCP Crime, Ahmedabad
‘I feel fortunate to have had good bosses who always supported me in ideas I proposed.’
I feel honoured that my boss (AK Singh) has put trust in my capabilities. I am fortunate to have had good bosses who always supported me in whatever idea I have proposed. However, I strongly believe success is not because of one person but of the entire team—it is team work. In the next 11 years, we want to bring about a change in the image of the police.
We want to create an environment where people don’t fear to approach the police. Secondly, we want to bring about sea-change in policing system in next five to six years. Fortunately, now we are getting well-educated young recruits. Though we have two mahila police stations in the city, with 33 per cent reservation for women in recruitment, we want to recruit more women and want female citizens to feel empowered to file complaints without hesitation.
We also want to improve police-people interaction. Learning technological tools for advancement is a must. It helps better while confronting the accused. Hence, more knowledge- sharing with police personnel will be on a priority. I don’t know where I will be in next 11 years as these things are not in our hands. But my idea is wherever you are posted, give your best. I want to have the same zeal for work after 11 years that I have today. Also I want to give more time to my family which I am not able to at the moment.
(As told to KULDEEP TIWARI)
